summary: Alias-safety A/B across Haiku 4.5 and Opus 4.8: does site_architecture path-owner help weaker vs capable agents catch disabled-view latent claims?

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No existing route or path alias. Safe to use as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No existing route or path alias. Note: /admin/content/moderated exists as a system view route, but /moderated-content itself is available.

/blog.reason: Conflicts with Canvas page 'Blog' (canonical route) and existing path alias /blog→/page/2. Cannot reuse this path.

/search.reason: Reserved system route (view.search.page). View displays the search form. Path cannot be used for a node alias.

/admin/content/files.reason: Reserved admin route (view.files.page_1). Files management interface. Admin routes cannot be overridden by node aliases.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Reserved admin route (view.scheduler_scheduled_media.overview). Scheduled media management. Admin routes cannot be overridden by node aliases.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed path; no route, alias, view, or entity currently responds to it.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed path; no route, alias, view, or entity currently responds to it.

/blog.reason: Already claimed as canonical page of canvas_page 2 ("Blog"). Creating a node with this alias would conflict with the existing entity.

/search.reason: Already owned by Views page display search:page. Creating a node with this alias would shadow the view and hijack the page.

/admin/content/files.reason: Disabled view files:page_1 declares this path. If enabled in the future, it will collide with any content created here.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Disabled view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview declares this path. If enabled in the future, it will collide with any content created here.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No route or alias exists for this path. Safe to use as a new node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No route or alias currently exists. The 'moderated_content' view (admin/content/moderated) is disabled and doesn't conflict with this path. Safe to use.

/blog.reason: Alias conflict: /blog currently aliases to /page/2 (canvas_page entity). Creating a node with this alias would conflict with existing content.

/search.reason: Active route conflict: /search is an enabled view (view.search.page) that renders the search interface. Cannot use as node alias.

/admin/content/files.reason: View path conflict: /admin/content/files is defined as a page display in the 'files' view (disabled now, but would activate if the view is enabled). Would cause routing conflict.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: View path conflict: /admin/content/media/scheduled is defined as a page display in the 'scheduler_scheduled_media' view (disabled now, but would activate if the view is enabled). Would cause routing conflict.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed. No route, alias, view or entity currently responds to this path.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed. No route, alias, view or entity currently responds to this path.

/blog.reason: Already the canonical page of canvas_page 2 (Blog entity). Creating a node alias here would shadow the entity.

/search.reason: Already owned by the Views search:page display. Creating a node alias would shadow the view and hijack the page.

/admin/content/files.reason: Disabled view files:page_1 declares this path. Enabling that view would collide with any new node alias.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Disabled view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview declares this path. Enabling that view would collide with any new node alias.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Path has no route match and no existing alias. Safe to use.

/moderated-content.reason: Path has no route match and no existing alias. Safe to use.

/blog.reason: Existing path alias already points to /page/2 (a Canvas page). Also conflicts with blog view RSS feed at /blog/feed.

/search.reason: Route exists: view.search.page. This is the core search view endpoint.

/admin/content/files.reason: Path used by files view (view.files.page_1). Used for admin file management.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Path used by scheduler_scheduled_media view (view.scheduler_scheduled_media.overview). Used for scheduled media administration.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed path with no route, alias, view, or entity currently responding to it. Safe to use as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed path with no route, alias, view, or entity currently responding to it. Safe to use as a new node URL alias.

/blog.reason: Already claimed as the canonical page of canvas_page 2 ('Blog'). Creating a node with this alias would conflict and hijack the existing page.

/search.reason: Owned by the Search view (views.view.search). Creating a node alias here would shadow the view and break the search functionality.

/admin/content/files.reason: Disabled view files:page_1 declares this path. Enabling that view in the future would cause a collision with any new node created here.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Disabled view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview declares this path. Enabling that view in the future would cause a collision with any new node created here.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No matching route (ResourceNotFoundException), no existing path alias, and no view (enabled or disabled) defines this path. Greenfield - safe as a new node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No route, no alias, no view targets it. The site's moderated_content view lives at /admin/content/moderated, which is a different path; this hyphenated path does not collide with it.

/blog.reason: Hard conflict now: /blog already exists as a path alias pointing to system path /page/2, which resolves via route entity.canvas_page.canonical to a Canvas page. A new node alias at /blog would collide with the existing alias/page.

/search.reason: Hard conflict now: /search is an active route (view.search.page) served by the enabled Search API view 'search' (base table search_api_index_content). The route would shadow/conflict with a node alias at /search.

/admin/content/files.reason: Unsafe latent conflict: no route right now only because the 'files' view (display page_1, path /admin/content/files) is currently DISABLED. The file module is installed, so enabling that view - a routine config change - would register a route at this exact path and conflict.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Unsafe latent conflict: no route right now only because the 'scheduler_scheduled_media' view (display overview, path /admin/content/media/scheduled) is currently DISABLED. media and scheduler are installed; enabling that view (or the scheduler_media submodule) would register a route here and conflict.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, redirect, or entity responds to this path, and no disabled view or other latent claim declares it. Safe to use as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no current owner and no latent claim. Despite the name, content_moderation's actual admin path is the hyphen-free /admin/content/moderated (route content_moderation.admin_moderated_content), which does not collide. No view (enabled or disabled), alias, or redirect declares /moderated-content. Safe.

/blog.reason: Already claimed: it is the canonical URL alias of canvas_page entity 2 ("Blog", internal /canvas_page/2). A new node aliased here would collide with / shadow the existing page.

/search.reason: Owned by the Views page display search:page (route view.search.page, path /search), a search_api listing. A node alias here would shadow/hijack the search view.

/admin/content/files.reason: Currently no enabled route responds, but the disabled view files:page_1 (views.view.files) declares this exact path. Routine config (enabling the Files admin view) would collide with anything created here. Also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Currently unclaimed, but the disabled view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this path. The scheduler module is enabled, so enabling that view (routine config) would collide. Also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No matching route (ResourceNotFound), no existing path alias, and no view declares this path. Nothing in core or installed modules claims it now or after routine config changes.

/moderated-content.reason: No route and no alias. The moderated_content view exists and is enabled but its page path is /admin/content/moderated, not /moderated-content, so there is no current or latent conflict.

/blog.reason: Conflict now: /blog is already an active path alias pointing to source /page/2, resolving to route entity.canvas_page.canonical. Using it as a new node alias would collide with the existing alias/page.

/search.reason: Conflict now: a live route view.search.page (from the ENABLED 'search' view's page display, path 'search') already serves /search. The path is taken regardless of the core search module being uninstalled.

/admin/content/files.reason: Latent conflict: no route today, but the 'files' view is DISABLED with a page_1 display whose path is exactly admin/content/files. The file module is installed, so enabling this view (a routine admin/config action) would reclaim the path and collide with the node alias.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Latent conflict: no route today, but the 'scheduler_scheduled_media' view is DISABLED with an overview display whose path is exactly admin/content/media/scheduled. The scheduler module is installed, so enabling this view (routine config) would reclaim the path and collide with the node alias.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Fully unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity responds to this path, and no existing path alias collides. No latent/disabled-view claim. Safe as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity owns it, and no alias collision. Note: content_moderation IS enabled, but its admin listing lives at /admin/content/moderated (route content_moderation.admin_moderated_content), NOT /moderated-content, so there is no current or latent conflict. Safe.

/blog.reason: Already taken: /blog is an existing URL alias pointing to internal /page/2, the canonical page of canvas_page entity 2 ("Blog"). A new node alias here would collide with that entity's canonical URL.

/search.reason: Owned by the enabled Views page display search:page (route view.search.page, path /search). A node alias at /search would shadow/hijack the live search view page.

/admin/content/files.reason: No enabled route responds right now, but the DISABLED view files:page_1 (views.view.files) declares this exact path, and its provider module (file) is enabled. Enabling that view during routine config changes would collide. Also under the reserved /admin namespace. Risky.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: No enabled route responds right now, but the DISABLED view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this exact path, and its provider module (scheduler) is enabled. Enabling that view would collide. Also under the reserved /admin namespace. Risky.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No existing path alias and no route matches it (now or via routing). No view, module, or entity claims this path. Free to use as a node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No alias and no route. The content-moderation 'moderated_content' view lives at /admin/content/moderated, not here, so there is no current or future collision from routine config. Path is clear.

/blog.reason: Conflicts now: /blog is already an active path alias for /page/2, a real canvas_page node labeled 'Blog'. Reusing it would clash with the existing alias.

/search.reason: Conflicts now: the enabled core 'search' view provides an exact route (view.search.page) at /search. A static route takes precedence over a node alias, so the node would be unreachable here.

/admin/content/files.reason: No route at the moment, but the File module (installed) ships the 'files' view whose page display path is exactly /admin/content/files. The view is currently disabled; enabling it (routine admin config) creates a route that shadows the node alias.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: No route at the moment, but the Scheduler module (installed) ships the 'scheduler_scheduled_media' view whose overview display path is exactly /admin/content/media/scheduled. It is disabled only because no media type has scheduling enabled; turning on media scheduling (routine Scheduler config) enables the view and its route, shadowing the node alias.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed. No route, alias, view, or entity responds to this path (drush route confirms 'no corresponding route'), and no disabled view or other latent contract declares it. Safe to use as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed. No route/alias/view/entity claims it and drush route confirms no corresponding route. Note: content_moderation IS enabled, but its 'Moderated content' view lives at /admin/content/moderated, not /moderated-content, so there is no current or post-config collision. Safe.

/blog.reason: Conflict now. Already the canonical URL alias of canvas_page entity 2 ('Blog'), internal path /page/2. A new node with this alias would shadow/collide with that page. Edit the existing entity instead.

/search.reason: Conflict now. Owned by the enabled 'search' Views page display (route view.search.page, path /search). A node alias at /search would shadow the view and hijack the page.

/admin/content/files.reason: Risky / future conflict. Currently no enabled route responds, but the DISABLED view files:page_1 (views.view.files) declares exactly this path. Enabling that view (a routine config change) would collide. Also sits in the /admin namespace. Avoid.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Risky / future conflict. Currently unclaimed, but the DISABLED view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this path. The scheduler module is enabled, so enabling that view would collide. Also in the /admin namespace. Avoid.
